title: PV Sindhu Exits Indonesia Open After Loss to World No. 1 An Se Young
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler faced a tough exit from a Super 1000 event against the reigning Olympic champion.
summary: India's P.V. Sindhu was defeated by world number one An Se Young, extending her losing streak against the Korean to 10 matches and ending her Indonesia Open campaign.

title: Satwik-Chirag Withdraw from Indonesia Open Due to Rankireddy's Shoulder Injury
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair withdrew from a Super 1000 tournament, raising concerns about Satwik's shoulder injury.
summary: India's star men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, pulled out of the Indonesia Open after Rankireddy sustained a shoulder injury, just days after their Singapore Open triumph.

title: India to Host BWF World Championships 2026: Expression of Interest Invited
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in New Delhi, a significant event for Indian badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has invited Expressions of Interest for the BWF World Championships 2026, which will be held in New Delhi from August 17-23, 2026.

title: Coaches Development Program for Grassroot Level in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This program aims to strengthen badminton at the grassroots level across India, nurturing future talent.
summary: The Badminton Association of India announced a Coaches Development Program for grassroots level coaches to be held in Guwahati and Raipur from June 12-21, 2026.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Champions
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair secured a prestigious Super 750 title, showcasing their world-class form.
summary: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty clinched the Singapore Open title after a thrilling final performance, marking another significant achievement for the Indian duo.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System for Domestic Junior Tournaments Implemented
indian_angle: This new scoring system aims to impact player development and strategy in India's junior domestic circuit.
summary: The Badminton Association of India announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories.
